concise_summary: PathoScope AI processed the viral genome sequence (GC content: 0.0%) and successfully predicted 13 open reading frames (average length: 88 aa). Homology searches identified 1 high-confidence Swiss-Prot matches and 0 Pfam domains, implicating 0 host biological pathway networks for downstream functional analysis.
detailed_biological_interpretation:
PathoScope AI conducted deterministic sequence QC and gene scanning. GC content is calculated at 0.0%, and sequence validation N50 score is 0 bp. A total of 13 open reading frames were predicted using frame-scanning, ranging in size 31-567 amino acids. 
Reference Database Homology: - ORF mapping to Swiss-Prot accession O11283 was detected with a bitscore of 1010.0 (Identity: 91.87%, Query Coverage: 100.0%). 
No host metabolic or signaling pathways were significantly implicated during enrichment calculations.
limitations: Statistical enrichment calculations are constrained by the background universe size settings and local Pfam database footprints. Dynamic pathways rely on Swiss-Prot accession cross-references in KEGG and Reactome networks. Homology alignments are limited to primary sequences, and three-dimensional structural configurations remain unmodeled. Hypothetical proteins lacking reference database alignments are excluded from pathway projection lists.
confidence_warnings: In silico predictions represent high-confidence functional hypotheses and require downstream in vitro laboratory validation.
A total of 12 predicted open reading frames failed reference database alignments and have been classified as hypothetical proteins.
